task_completion.description: Set the HU threshold to 200-3071 and generate the first 3D surface.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The InVesalius threshold panel shows a minimum of 200 and maximum of 3071, and a 3D surface of the skull is generated in the 3D viewer.

task_completion.description: Export the 200 HU surface as a binary STL file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An export/save dialog is visible with the file path '/home/ga/Documents/skull_200HU.stl' and the STL format selected.

task_completion.description: Set the HU threshold to 400-3071 and generate the second 3D surface.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The InVesalius threshold panel shows a minimum of 400 and maximum of 3071, and a new 3D surface is generated in the 3D viewer.

task_completion.description: Export the 400 HU surface as a binary STL file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An export/save dialog is visible with the file path '/home/ga/Documents/skull_400HU.stl' and the STL format selected.

task_completion.description: Set the HU threshold to 600-3071 and generate the third 3D surface.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The InVesalius threshold panel shows a minimum of 600 and maximum of 3071, and a new 3D surface is generated in the 3D viewer.

task_completion.description: Export the 600 HU surface as a binary STL file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An export/save dialog is visible with the file path '/home/ga/Documents/skull_600HU.stl' and the STL format selected.

integrity.description: The agent used the InVesalius 3 GUI to perform the thresholding and exporting.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show active interaction with the InVesalius application windows, menus, and dialogs, rather than terminal commands or scripts generating the files.

integrity.description: The agent genuinely generated different surfaces for each threshold.
integrity.visual_evidence: The 3D surface in the viewer visibly changes (becomes sparser, showing less tissue/noise) as the threshold increases from 200 to 400 to 600 HU.

integrity.description: The agent used the proper 3D surface export functionality.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent uses the 'Export 3D surface' or 'Save As' dialog specific to the 3D surface module in InVesalius, ensuring the output is an actual 3D model and not a 2D slice or project file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires exporting three binary STL files from the CT Cranium dataset using InVesalius 3. The minimum Hounsfield Unit (HU) thresholds must be set to 200, 400, and 600, with a constant maximum of 3071 HU. As the threshold increases from 200 to 600 HU, the generated 3D surface should visibly decrease in volume and triangle count (lower thresholds include more tissue, higher thresholds exclude noise). The files must be saved to /home/ga/Documents/ with the exact names skull_200HU.stl, skull_400HU.stl, and skull_600HU.stl.
